calendar.matters:
Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement, And For Dismissal With Prejudice Of First Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust, Etc.
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cross for (petitioner) Arnold King.

The Court stated that it has read the settlement agreement and asked if there was anything further to report. Mr. Cross reported that there is a hearing being held in the parallel case in San Mateo County this morning and that the 2 orders and 2 motions combined complete the agreement. The Court stated that based on the consent of the parties the Court finds the Settlement Agreement ready to be granted, that once the petition is granted the other petition can be taken off calendar. Mr. Cross stated that they will file a dismissal this week. The Court granted the petition for approval of settlement agreement, and for dismissal with prejudice of first amended petition to determine validity of purported trust, etc., and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cross for Arnold King.

Mr. Cross reported that Mr. Madden is out of town and that he is appearing for both of them, that the settlement agreement has been signed and that motions are ready to be filed in the next day or two. The Court reminded Mr. Cross that for probate it will be a petition to approve the settlement agreement not a motion. The petition to determine validity of purported trust, etc., was continued to May 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Continued To Apr-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross Madden for Dick Chang & Chuan Chang. Robert Cross for (petitioner) Arnold King.

Mr. Madden reported that mediation was scheduled for December, that a few days beforehand he was told that it would not happen, new counsel was obtained and that mediation is now set for March. Mr. Cross reported that his client is in Hong Kong, that he will be available in late March for mediation and that there is also a pending petition in Hong Kong to appoint Mr. King regarding the Hong Kong will. Mr. Madden stated that he is ready, just waiting for the other side. The Court stated that it will continue the matter, that it has been continued many times always due to the clients being in Hong Kong, that this has to either be resolved or tried and that if it is not resolved when they come back they are to be prepared to talk about the issues and how they plan to resolve them. The petition to determine validity of purported trust, etc., was continued to April 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Siu. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Braccini for (petitioner) Arnold King. Ross Madden for Dick Chang.

The Court asked counsel for an update. Mr. Madden stated that they have been to mediation and are pending resumption, that he just found out that Mr. Braccini needs additional information, that Katherine Gallagher is the mediator and that they will schedule the nest session quickly. Mr. Braccini stated that the case should be mediated by the first of the year. The petition to determine validity of purported trust, etc., was continued to January 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status of resolution. The parties are to meet and confer prior to this date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Continued To Oct-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Cunningham (by CourtCall) for Petitioner Arnold King; Ross Madden for Respondent Chuan Chang

Mr. Madden reported that the parties had agreed to mediation, and that the mediation was set to be held before Katherine Gallagher on Monday, July 20th. He requested that the hearing on the petition be continued for 60-90 days to permit the mediation to go forward, and to give the parties time to conduct some discovery in the event the mediation was not successful. Mr. Cunningham agreed with the request. The Court continued the hearing on the Petition to Determine Validity of Purported trust; to Impose Constructive Trust, and to Direct Transfer of Property and set a status hearing regarding the mediation for October 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determin Validity Of Purported Trust; To Impose Constructive Trust; And For Order Directing Transfer Of Property To The Prior Trust
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Continued To Jul-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Rodgriguez for (Petitioner) Arnold King. Ross Madden for (Respondents) Dick Leung Chang & Chuan Fang Chang.

The Court stated that there are competing positions regarding the validity of the trust and the powers of the parties and asked if they had a procedure to resolve. Mr. Rodriguez reported that the parties have not talked, that they are open to private mediation, that they would like to start discovery with the thought that it will help to narrow and clarify some issues and requested a continuance of 90-120 days. Mr. Rodriguez requested suspension of the trustees powers (which the court is not inclined to do) and in the alternative requested court supervision to oversee the use of trust assets, concerned that the current trustee who is the spouse of a beneficiary could liquidate assets and make a distribution to the spouse. Mr. Madden stated that his clients will not liquidate the assets, they will act prudently, they have been managing the properties for 30 years and are happy to work with opposing counsel. Mr. Rodriguez stated that they have no objection to the will in Hong Kong and that once it is approved they will file a probate here. Mr. Madden stated that his client should have been notified of the probate in Hong Kong, he does not believe there is an actual will and that they are filing a probate on an earlier will. The Court stated that the trustee is bound by the rules of the trust, their fiduciary duties, all of which is subject to being addressed in an appropriate forum. The Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust, etc., was continued to July 8, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the parties should be prepared to report on the status of pursuing mediation and have met and conferred as to procedures and schedules going forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter: Connie Parchman, CSR #6137, 510-853-5260 (Reported)
